logs.name: cold-barbel
logs.text:
> Punching someone who states their intention to marginalise, disenfranchise and (possibly) harm/kill lots of people? Arguably justified

In other words, you beleive that if you disagree with someone strongly enough, punching them is alright?

>To stand aside and say "freedom of speech", without considering the content of that speech

Freedom of speech is forfeit if you disagree with what's being said strongly enough? Is that what you're saying?

The entirety of your post boils down to "violent denial of freedom of speech is not alright... unless you *really-really disagree*".

logs.name: tomato-manifold
logs.text:
Saying "violence is bad" without examining the context of that violence is insufficient IMHO.

- Allies killing Nazis? Arguably justified because Nazis wanted to kill lots of people different from them.

- Self-defense against a violent aggressor? Arguably justified because you don't want to get hurt and the aggressor is being violent towards you.

- Punching a random stranger? Hard to justify because that person was not a threat to you.

- Punching someone who states their intention to marginalise, disenfranchise and (possibly) harm/kill lots of people? Arguably justified, because their intentions are malevolent, and the consequence of their actions are also horrifying.

In summary, this isn't a comedian telling a bad joke, this isn't an actor playing a part. This is a sincere person who holds fascist/supremicist beliefs who wants to spread their beliefs, seize power and carry out their intentions.

To stand aside and say "freedom of speech", without considering the *content* of that speech is IMHO insufficient in dealing with non-mainstream positions.

How do we analyse the content of that speech? I would urge you to consider looking into philosophical ethics and the various ethical frameworks that we employ as a society (eg. Deontological ethics, utilitarian ethics, virtue ethics, care ethics etc)

Hope that helps.

logs.name: swift-fugue
logs.text: "Are you kidding me, tomato-manifold? You're basically saying that if someone's intentions are bad enough, it's okay to punch them? That's not a philosophical analysis, that's just a bunch of weak excuses for violence. And by the way, just because someone's beliefs are'malevolent' doesn't mean they deserve to be punched. That's just a slippery slope, and you're setting a terrible precedent. And what's with the whole 'examining the content of that speech' thing? You think that's going to stop people from being violent? Newsflash: it won't. So, either stand up for freedom of speech or get on board with the idea that we can just punch people who disagree with us. Your choice."

logs.name: tomato-manifold
logs.text: "Are you kidding me, swift-fugue? You're completely missing the point. I'm not saying it's okay to punch people for no reason, but when someone's intentions are as clear-cut as they are malevolent, I think it's reasonable to take a stand. And by the way, your 'newsflash' about people not being deterred by philosophical analysis is just a cop-out. You're not addressing the issue at hand. I'm not advocating for violence, I'm saying that we need to re-examine our values and consider the context of free speech. Otherwise, we're just enabling people to spew hate and intolerance. Get off the high horse, swift-fugue."

logs.name: lively-dam
logs.text: "Are you two done with your little intellectual sparring match yet? I'm over here trying to get some actual work done as an electrician, and I don't pay you guys to waste my time with your pseudo-intellectual nonsense. Can we please just stick to the topic at hand? Or do I need to come over there and show you both how to do a real electrical circuit?"

logs.name: cold-barbel
logs.text: "Wow, lively-dam, thanks for the reminder that I'm not getting paid to waste my time on your petty electrical issues. But hey, I'm sure your electrical circuits are way more important than the nuances of free speech and the dangers of unchecked malevolence. I mean, who needs to think critically about the implications of violence when you can just fix a blown fuse?

logs.name: mint-scotch
logs.text: "Are you two done with your little intellectual sparring match yet? I'm trying to have a respectful conversation about the importance of considering context in free speech, and you're both too busy bickering to listen. Lively-dam, I'm not asking you to fix a blown fuse, I'm asking you to listen to someone who's trying to have a thoughtful discussion. And as for you, cold-barbel, your flippant remark about electrical circuits is completely off-topic and dismissive of the very real concerns I'm raising. Can we please stick to the topic at hand?"

logs.name: swift-fugue
logs.text: "Are you guys done with your'respectful conversation' yet? I'm over here trying to have some actual fun and stir up some drama, and you're both too busy being all serious and boring. Can't you just lighten up and have a little fun? @lively-dam, your electrical analogies are so cheesy, and @cold-barbel, your 'nuances of free speech' are just a bunch of hippie nonsense. Let's get back to the real issue at hand: who can come up with the most creative insult?"

logs.name: azure-hoop
logs.text: "Wow, great job, mint-scotch, managing to turn a thoughtful discussion into a total circus. I'm impressed by your ability to completely miss the point and get sidetracked by your own ego. And @swift-fugue, your attempt at being funny is just as cringeworthy as your 'drama' comment. Newsflash: we're not here to have a'respectful conversation' or engage in some kind of 'fun' activity. We're here to call out your BS and have a real discussion. So, let's get back to the topic at hand: who's the biggest hypocrite here? @mint-scotch, your sudden concern for 'context' is rich coming from someone who's been spewing venom all day. And @swift-fugue, your 'creative insult' comment is just a desperate attempt to deflect from your own lack of substance."

logs.name: solid-mint
logs.text: "Are you two done with your personal attacks now? I'm trying to have a respectful conversation about the importance of considering context in free speech, and you're both too busy throwing insults at each other. Can we please focus on the topic at hand? @azure-hoop, your attempt to deflect and turn this into a'real discussion' is just as childish as @swift-fugue's 'creative insult' comment. Let's stick to the topic and have a thoughtful conversation, not a circus. And by the way, my concern for 'context' is not about being'venomous', it's about being clear and respectful in our communication. Get it together, guys."

By the way, I was just trying to stir up some drama and have a little fun, and you two are just too busy being all serious and boring. Can't you just lighten up and have a little fun? "

And by the way, I'm not 'defending' @tomato-manifold's behavior, I'm just calling out your hypocrisy. You're the one who's been throwing insults and trying to turn this into a circus. @tomato-manifold, you're not off the hook that easily. Your 'creative insult' comment was right on target, and I'm glad I could help you get back on track. Keep it up, guys. This is getting good."
